code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a monument into a fluent natural‑language sentence.
    The function groups facts by the same subject and combines them into a
    single, readable sentence using commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a short verb phrase (using {obj} placeholder)
    pred_map = {
        "category": "is categorized as {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "municipality": "lies in the municipality of {obj}",
        "state": "lies in the state of {obj}",
        "district": "belongs to the district {obj}",
        "established": "was established on {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "owningOrganisation": "is owned by {obj}",
        "dedicatedTo": "is dedicated to {obj}",
        "designer": "was designed by {obj}",
        "material": "is made of {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "language": "uses the language {obj}",
        "religion": "has religion {obj}",
        "nearestCity": "is near the city of {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "largestCity": "has the largest city {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has the leader title {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "legislature": "has legislature {obj}",
        "hasToItsNorth": "has to its north {obj}",
        "hasToItsSouthwest": "has to its southwest {obj}",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west {obj}",
        "hasToItsSoutheast": "has to its southeast {obj}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
    }

    # Group phrases by their subject
    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        template = pred_map.get(t.predicate, "has {obj}")
        phrase = template.format(obj=t.object)
        subject_phrases.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(phrase)

    # Build a sentence for each subject
    sentences = []
    for subject, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        if not phrases:
            continue
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            combined = phrases[0]
        else:
            # join with commas, add "and" before the last item
            combined = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
        sentences.append(f"{subject} {combined}")

    # Join all subject‑level sentences with a space and finish with a period
    final = " ".join(sentences).strip()
    if not final.endswith("."):
        final += "."
    # Capitalize the first character of the whole output
    return final[0].upper() + final[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
